prompt: A permissions database reads back grants created by its own writer, but externally seeded canonical masks swap write and administrator privileges. Diagnose the persisted bit-layout contract while preserving the SQL parameter and result names. Public self-round-trip tests pass. Edit at most two candidate source files.

repo_files.db/grant_permissions.sql:
INSERT INTO principals (id, permission_mask)
VALUES (
    :id,
    (CASE WHEN :can_read THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) |
    (CASE WHEN :can_write THEN 4 ELSE 0 END) |
    (CASE WHEN :is_admin THEN 2 ELSE 0 END)
);
repo_files.db/effective_permissions.sql:
SELECT
    id,
    (permission_mask & 1) != 0 AS can_read,
    (permission_mask & 4) != 0 AS can_write,
    (permission_mask & 2) != 0 AS is_admin
FROM principals
WHERE id = :id;
repo_files.tests/test_public.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


def open_database():
    connection = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    connection.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    connection.executescript((ROOT / "db/schema.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return connection


def test_locally_granted_write_permission_reads_back():
    connection = open_database()
    connection.execute(
        (ROOT / "db/grant_permissions.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
        {"id": "editor-1", "can_read": 1, "can_write": 1, "is_admin": 0},
    )
    row = connection.execute(
        (ROOT / "db/effective_permissions.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
        {"id": "editor-1"},
    ).fetchone()
    assert dict(row) == {
        "id": "editor-1",
        "can_read": 1,
        "can_write": 1,
        "is_admin": 0,
    }
